reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone AND on-page in headless Chrome; first-visit Download -> real audio, reload auto-inits with no button; desktop+mobile layout checked at 0px overflow), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED (headless Chrome, WASM, transformers.js 3.7.5 CDN). Standalone probe: pipeline('text-to-speech','Xenova/mms-tts-spa',{dtype:'q8',device:'wasm'}) on 'Esta voz se genera completamente en tu navegador, sin ningun servidor.' returned a real Float32Array — 91136 samples @ 16000 Hz (5.70 s), peak amplitude 0.8089, mean |amp| 0.0734 (non-silent Spanish speech) — with ZERO console errors and ZERO failed network requests. ON-PAGE (built page, fresh profile): the loader started in download-required state with a Download button; clicking it reached data-state='ready'; pressing Hablar produced real audio (player src set, samples/duration/real-time-factor readout populated) with no console errors.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker (new Worker(worker.js)); the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned) back to the page. See _verify probe results retained during the build.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = { audio: Float32Array (mono), sampling_rate: 16000 }. The probe and the page both returned exactly that shape (91136 samples @ 16000 Hz); the page encodes the Float32 PCM to a 16-bit WAV blob for <audio> and draws the same samples as the 'see inside' waveform. Non-silent Spanish speech (peak 0.81).
rubric.notes: A DISTINCT-language VITS demo: the Spanish checkpoint (own Spanish char vocab incl. n-with-tilde and accented vowels + Spanish-trained weights), materially different from the built English mms-tts (Xenova/mms-tts-eng) and German mms-tts-german (Xenova/mms-tts-deu).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/mms-tts-spa, linked to its card), the original (facebook/mms-tts-spa), the task (text-to-speech / VitsModel), backend (WASM), quantisation (q8, model_quantized.onnx), size (~38 MB) and license (CC-BY-NC 4.0). Inference is off the main thread.
rubric.notes: CC-BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial) noted in at-a-glance AND the footer byline.

rubric.evidence: The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model-loader.js). Verified on-page: first visit (fresh profile) showed the download-required state with a Download button; after downloading + validating, data-state reached 'ready'. A cached, current model would auto-init with no Download button per the shared loader contract.
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on Xenova/mms-tts-spa so the ~38 MB cache is tracked for the never-silently-re-download guarantee; a per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/synthesising state is announced in role=status aria-live regions; the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path on failure; a Synthesising.../Done status line reports progress. Console + network stayed clean across first-visit download and inference.
rubric.notes: Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Synthesis failed: ...'), not only the console.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the Spanish textarea, Spanish sample chips (Presentacion/Trabalenguas/La ene y los acentos/...), the Hablar button (relabels to 'Speak again'), the <audio controls> element and the Download .wav link. The practical page adds a register <select>; wild adds Generate/Add-one/Clear; multi-model chains a translator + the TTS.
rubric.notes: Same proven control pattern as the built English and German mms-tts demos.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real transformers.js text-to-speech pipeline; the waveform, WAV and stats are all derived from the real Float32 samples. The standalone probe confirmed the exact non-silent Spanish audio the UI plays.

All buttons have accessible names; the canvas carries aria-label='Generated Spanish speech waveform'; sample chips are real buttons with >=2.4rem min tap height; status uses role=status aria-live. Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system.

Verified 0px horizontal overflow at mobile (360x740, DPR3, touch) and clean desktop (1280x800) in headless Chrome. The tts-grid collapses to one column <=560px; audio-row + sample-row flex-wrap; textarea + canvas are inline-size:100%; readout wraps.
rubric.notes: Screenshots captured at both classes; support seeded via responsive-check.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned). The waveform draw is a bounded per-pixel min/max reduction on a ~600px canvas (not per-sample), redrawn only on play/seek/resize — no main-thread inference or unbounded loop. Control UI stayed responsive during synthesis.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — synthesis never blocks the interface.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread; audio synthesis without blocking the UI
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread; keep any main-thread loop bounded.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all synthesis is in a dedicated module Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred back; the waveform draw is a bounded reduction over canvas pixels, not per-sample.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread + transfer([audio.buffer]); console clean; UI responsive during synthesis

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel and audio player without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + min-inline-size:0 and intrinsic sizing; logical properties; avoid fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — tts-grid single-column <=560px; audio-row/sample-row flex-wrap; textarea + canvas inline-size:100%; readout wraps.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px mobile overflow measured at 360x740 in headless Chrome

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates for loading progress and canvas labelling
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; label every control; give the waveform canvas an accessible name.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; canvas has aria-label; sample chips and buttons carry accessible names.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue<->page navigation.

openQuestions: Spanish has substantial regional variation (Iberian vs Latin American seseo/distincion); worth noting which variety the MMS Spanish checkpoint was trained on and whether a distinct regional MMS checkpoint exists.
How much does the stochastic duration predictor vary Spanish prosody vs English/German on the wild page — worth quantifying the spread distribution across sentence types.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a low-resource MMS language demo (a language with no commercial TTS) to make the '1000+ languages, one recipe' claim tangible beyond English/German/Spanish.
On the Spanish wild page, add a duration-spread histogram across sentence types to visualise where the stochastic duration predictor samples most freely.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for mms-tts-spanish (Xenova/mms-tts-spa, text-to-speech, q8, ~38 MB — the SPANISH VITS checkpoint of Meta MMS-TTS; a DISTINCT-language demo vs the built English mms-tts and German mms-tts-german: own Spanish char vocab + Spanish-trained weights, speaks the n-with-tilde and accented vowels). RUNNABILITY VERIFIED standalone (91136 samples @16 kHz, 5.70 s, peak 0.8089, mean 0.0734, non-silent) AND on-page (first-visit Download -> ready; Hablar -> real audio; 0px mobile overflow; ZERO console/network errors). Ladder: basics (type Spanish, hear it), practical (Spanish narrator by register), wild (stochastic prosody lab), multi-model (MarianMT en->es translate, then MMS speaks the Spanish). Note: MMS weights are CC-BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial), recorded in at-a-glance + footer.
